([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): ‎کننگہام گھنٹہ گھر) in [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), [Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), was built in 1900,[[1]](#cite_note-1) "in commemoration of [Her Majesty the Queen Empress](/wiki/Diamond_Jubilee_of_Queen_Victoria "Diamond Jubilee of Queen Victoria") [Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria")". The tower was named after the Commissioner of Peshawar, Sir Alexander Frederick Douglas Cunningham[[2]](#cite_note-2) (son of [Alexander Cunningham](/wiki/Alexander_Cunningham "Alexander Cunningham")), not to be confused with [Sir George Cunningham](/wiki/Sir_George_Cunningham "Sir George Cunningham"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) former British [governor](/wiki/List_of_Governors_of_the_North-West_Frontier_Province "List of Governors of the North-West Frontier Province") and political agent in the province.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [34°00′35″N 71°34′33″E﻿ / ﻿34.009846°N 71.575784°E﻿ / 34.009846; 71.575784](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Cunningham_Clock_Tower&params=34.009846_N_71.575784_E_type:landmark_region:PK)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British-era tower in Peshawar, Pakistan

Cunningham Clock Tower

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| کننگہام گھنٹہ گھر | |
|  | |
| [Map](/wiki/Special:Map/13/34.009846/71.575784/en) Interactive map of Cunningham Clock Tower | |
| Location | [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Coordinates | [34°00′35″N 71°34′33″E﻿ / ﻿34.009846°N 71.575784°E﻿ / 34.009846; 71.575784](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Cunningham_Clock_Tower&params=34.009846_N_71.575784_E_type:landmark_region:PK) |
| Type | Clock tower |
| Completion date | 1900 |

The **Cunningham Clock Tower** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): ‎کننگہام گھنٹہ گھر) in [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), [Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), was built in 1900,[[1]](#cite_note-1) "in commemoration of [Her Majesty the Queen Empress](/wiki/Diamond_Jubilee_of_Queen_Victoria "Diamond Jubilee of Queen Victoria") [Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria")". The tower was named after the Commissioner of Peshawar, Sir Alexander Frederick Douglas Cunningham[[2]](#cite_note-2) (son of [Alexander Cunningham](/wiki/Alexander_Cunningham "Alexander Cunningham")), not to be confused with [Sir George Cunningham](/wiki/Sir_George_Cunningham "Sir George Cunningham"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) former British [governor](/wiki/List_of_Governors_of_the_North-West_Frontier_Province "List of Governors of the North-West Frontier Province") and political agent in the province.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cunningham_Clock_Tower&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Designed by James Strachan, the Municipal Engineer of [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), the foundation stone was laid by Cunningham, commissioner of Peshawar in 1898. It was opened to the public in 1900 to commemorate the [Diamond Jubilee of Queen Victoria](/wiki/Diamond_Jubilee_of_Queen_Victoria "Diamond Jubilee of Queen Victoria"). The construction of the building was funded by Balmukand Ahooja - Banker and Contractor of Peshawar [[4]](#cite_note-youlin-4)

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cunningham_Clock_Tower&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Structure")]

The tower is 31 feet in diameter. Its base is 13 by 4 metres (43 ft × 13 ft) and stands 26 metres (85 ft) tall at the Ghanta Ghar Chowk ("Clock Tower Square").

## Gallery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cunningham_Clock_Tower&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Gallery")]

* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Cunningham_clock_tower_Peshawar.jpg/120px-Cunningham_clock_tower_Peshawar.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cunningham_clock_tower_Peshawar.jpg)
